summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirmed that both non-blocking parser variants accept an oversized document when the final feedInput() chunk crosses the configured maxDocumentLength, while the fixed-buffer parser and 1 KB chunked async feeds reject the same input.
expected_security_impact: The reproduction should show a document larger than maxDocumentLength rejected on the fixed-buffer parser and on 1 KB-chunk async feeds, but accepted on both non-blocking parser variants when delivered in a single final chunk.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh cloned commit 22815c439ec1769a60f43c2b971d0cc31f382536, built the reproduction image, and confirmed that the fixed-buffer parser rejected the oversized document while both non-blocking parser variants accepted it in one chunk and rejected it again when the same document was fed in 1 KB chunks.
verification_method: reproduction/verify.sh compiles the target source tree directly, runs AsyncDocumentLengthHarness, and verifies the exact accepted/rejected status lines with grep.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container.
